step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the initial assessment. The use of acid etching significantly improves marginal adaptation because it creates micromechanical bonds between the composite material and the dental structure, enhancing both mechanical interlocking and chemical bonding. This method provides a stronger seal compared to other approaches such as simply controlling moisture conditions, which may still allow microleakage due to poor contact at margins.

The creation of these micromechanical bonds through acid etching ensures better integration of the filling material into the surrounding tooth tissue, reducing gaps and improving long-term durability against wear and decay. Therefore, my answer remains consistent with option C.
